prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (CH₂O) computed at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz, single-point (no re-optimization). Fukui indices derived from finite-difference Mulliken partial charges over the neutral (N), cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states.

Headline result — most electrophilic site: Carbon (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.7083913. Most nucleophilic site: Oxygen (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742464. Full per-atom results: O(1): f⁺=0.46814, f⁻=0.46742, f⁰=0.46778, Δf=+0.000714; C(2): f⁺=0.70839, f⁻=0.11244, f⁰=0.41042, Δf=+0.59595; H(3): f⁺=−0.08827, f⁻=0.21008, f⁰=0.06090, Δf=−0.29835; H(4): f⁺=−0.08826, f⁻=0.21006, f⁰=0.06090, Δf=−0.29832. Charge conservation checks: sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f−)=1.0000 — both passed. Integrity trustworthy: true. No warnings reported (empty warnings array). The engine integrity checks all passed (fukui_charge_conservation_f+, fukui_charge_conservation_f−, schema_header — all ok:true).
